releaseNotes: v0.33.0 — xlsx + PDF import for the Liberty Stock report. Bumpus's 2026-05-27 sample of Liberty's 'Current Stock' export arrived as xlsx, not CSV; v0.31 only accepted CSV. v0.33 extends the importer to accept all three formats. The importer dispatches on filename extension (with magic-byte fallback) and routes through the same PHI guard + NDC resolution + reconciliation logic regardless of format. xlsx parsing is hand-rolled on the existing archive + xml deps (~165 lines; avoids dep conflicts with the mainstream xlsx packages). PDF parsing uses Syncfusion's natively-Dart PDF library — extracts text words with bounds, re-clusters into logical rows by Y-coordinate (rolling 3pt tolerance, robust to baseline jitter), and assigns each word to a column using the header word's left edge as the column boundary (so long drug names don't get their trailing words stolen by neighboring columns). Three-reviewer audit (R1 self + R2 Sonnet + R3 Codex) found and fixed a P1 (UTF-8 decoding bug in the xlsx reader — flagged independently by both Sonnet and Codex; pre-fix drug names with em dashes or accents would have mojibake'd silently) plus 3 P2s (PDF Y-clustering robustness, long drug-name column assignment, defensive shared-string OOB handling). Also added a hostile DOCTYPE regression test to prove the XML parser doesn't follow external entity references. 679 tests passing (+23 vs v0.32.0), including 22 dedicated to xlsx + PDF format detection and round-trip + 4 tests using Bumpus's actual 2026-05-27 file as a fixture. CSV import remains the default and is byte-identical to v0.32 behavior. Auto-updater rolls v0.32.0 → v0.33.0 on next launch.
